content:
Hi! I'm quite new to this topic, so I want to share one problem with you, looking for any advice.
I want to create a kernel module that uses a no-std compatible crate from libcrux. For doing that, I'm currently building a static lib using cargo build, which produces the .a library that I need. I've tried to build from a Makefile both files (the static library and module.rs, the file with the module declaration), and it seems ok. But since I also need to export some symbols from the library, I've created a C file with something like this:

extern void foo_rust(void);
void foo(){
    foo_rust();
}
EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(foo);

But now, when I add that file to the Makefile, I'm getting several modpost errors.

It is hard to say what you are trying to do (e.g. what is linked to what and where) -- just in case, please note that it is very unlikely that the kernel will ever support arbitrary Cargo crates. I imagine this is all out-of-tree, but still, I am mentioning it just in case. Similarly, I should make the standard note that Rust for Linux's focus is in-tree development, not custom systems outside the kernel.
